fields.title: MeshCtrl.js Filepath
fields.caption: The file path to meshctrl.js . This is the javascript file from MeshCentral needed to execute remote commands on the MeshCentral server.

fields.title: MeshCentral Device List CSV File Path
fields.caption: Folder path for temporary CSV file to parse list of endpoints

fields.title: MeshCentral API Username
fields.caption: Username to access the MeshCentral API.

fields.title: MeshCentral API Password
fields.caption: Password to access the MeshCentral API.

fields.title: MeshCentral WSS URL
fields.caption: URL of the MeshCentral server starting with wss://

fields.title: Message
fields.caption: The message to send to the endpoints.

fields.title: Filter
fields.caption: The filter to use to find your list of MeshCentral devices to send the message to. Refer to meshctrl.js documentation from the MeshCentral developers.

params.script:
#!/bin/bash

# Enter your shell script code here

#generate variable for temp CSV filename with timestamp
tmp_filename_timestamp=$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S_%3N)

#create temporary CSV file with header
echo "group_id,group_name,device_id,device_name,icon_type,connection_status,power_status" > {{ workflow.params.MeshCSVFolderLocation }}/MeshDeviceTempCSV_${tmp_filename_timestamp}.csv

#go through list of devices using the filter and append to temporary CSV file
node {{ workflow.params.MeshCtrlJsPath }} --url {{ workflow.params.MeshCentralURL }} --loginuser {{ workflow.params.MeshAPIUsername }} --loginpass {{ workflow.params.MeshAPIPassword }} ListDevices --csv --filter {{ workflow.params.MeshFilter }} >> {{ workflow.params.MeshCSVFolderLocation }}/MeshDeviceTempCSV_${tmp_filename_timestamp}.csv

#remove the double quotes from the CSV
sed 's/"//g' {{ workflow.params.MeshCSVFolderLocation }}/MeshDeviceTempCSV_${tmp_filename_timestamp}.csv > {{ workflow.params.MeshCSVFolderLocation }}/MeshDeviceTempCSV_${tmp_filename_timestamp}_noquotes.csv

#load CSV_FILE_PATH variable for parsing through endpoints in while loop
CSV_FILE_PATH={{ workflow.params.MeshCSVFolderLocation }}/MeshDeviceTempCSV_${tmp_filename_timestamp}_noquotes.csv

# Read the entire file content into the 'CSV_DATA' variable
CSV_DATA=$(<"$CSV_FILE_PATH")

# Loop through the endpoints
# The 'while read' loop reads the data line by line.
# IFS=',' temporarily sets the field separator to a comma for the read command.
# -r prevents backslashes from being interpreted as escape characters.

while IFS=',' read -r col1 col2 col3 col4 col5 col6 col7
do
  # Skip the header line (simple check for "group_id")
  if [[ "$col1" == "group_id" ]]; then
    continue
  fi
       
  # Process endpoint with message command
  echo "Running command on endpoint $col4 with ID $col3"
  node {{ workflow.params.MeshCtrlJsPath }} --url "{{ workflow.params.MeshCentralURL }}" --loginuser {{ workflow.params.MeshAPIUsername }} --loginpass {{ workflow.params.MeshAPIPassword }} DeviceMessage --id $col3 --msg "{{ workflow.params.Message }}" --title "{{ workflow.params.Title }}"


done <<< "$CSV_DATA" # This feeds the variable content to the while loop

#remove the csv when done
rm -f {{ workflow.params.MeshCSVFolderLocation }}/MeshDeviceTempCSV_${tmp_filename_timestamp}.csv

#remove the csv file with no quotes when done
rm -f {{ workflow.params.MeshCSVFolderLocation }}/MeshDeviceTempCSV_${tmp_filename_timestamp}_noquotes.csv
